header.service: DEMO — UC San Diego Staff Performance Appraisal (SPA)
header.scope: Setting expectations → Signed appraisal filed in personnel file
header.lead: A. Practice Lead, Director of AI Strategy

personDoes.value: Reviews their job description and agrees standards and goals with their leader.

frontstage.value: Leader holds the goal-setting meeting and walks through the updated job description.

backstage.value: Leader pulls the prior job description, checks whether duties changed, consults HR on standards.

systems.value: SPA; job description record; UC Learning Center (supervisor training).

stageRows.stage: Doing the work and gathering evidence

personDoes.value: Performs the year's work; informally tracks accomplishments.

frontstage.value: Leader gives ongoing feedback and any mid-year check-ins.

backstage.value: Leader observes performance and keeps notes toward the eventual draft.

personDoes.value: Completes and submits the self-appraisal in SPA (optional).

frontstage.value: Leader sets a self-appraisal deadline and sends reminders.

backstage.value: HR sends cycle-open notifications; verifies training-assignment status.

stageRows.stage: Getting the draft and talking it through

personDoes.value: Receives the draft and discusses it in the appraisal meeting.

frontstage.value: Leader drafts the appraisal (Model A or B) and holds the meeting.

backstage.value: Draft sits in 'Supervisor Draft' status, visible only to the leader, indirect supervisors, HR, and the business officer.

systems.value: SPA (Model A / Model B); the employee's submitted self-appraisal.

personDoes.value: Adds comments, then waits while the appraisal is in 'HR Review' and can no longer edit.

frontstage.value: Leader confirms the employee had their comment window; answers status questions.

backstage.value: Appraisal moves to 'HR Review'; HR, leader, and indirect supervisors can view; HR checks compliance and clears it.

personDoes.value: Signs the appraisal and receives a copy.

frontstage.value: Leader collects required signatures and provides the copy to the employee.

backstage.value: Business officer assists signature routing; signed document filed in the personnel file; feeds merit decisions.

handoffs.whatBreaks: If skipped (optional), the leader drafts without it; the year is reconstructed from memory.

handoffs.whatMoves: Completed draft moved to 'HR Review' status
handoffs.whatBreaks: Employee loses visibility and edit access; no status is surfaced back, so it reads as a black box.
During the late-cycle flood, clearance queues; delay is read as HR being slow.

handoffs.whatMoves: Final signed copy of the appraisal
handoffs.whatBreaks: Copy delayed when signatures are still outstanding.

handoffs.how: Manual signature routing by email, then filing
handoffs.whatBreaks: Signatures chased one by one; filing slips past the deadline.

decisions.stage: Getting the draft and talking it through
decisions.decision: Which appraisal model to use (Model A or Model B)
decisions.decidesOn: Whether the employee is represented (union); department norm
decisions.basis: Represented staff may use the union-specific Model B, selected with HR assistance; others use Model A
decisions.failurePath: Wrong model chosen; HR re-routes, draft redone

decisions.decision: Whether the employee is included in this annual cycle
decisions.decidesOn: Probationary status; hire date relative to April 30
decisions.basis: Probationary employees get a separate probationary appraisal; staff hired after April 30 are not required to self-appraise this cycle
decisions.failurePath: Someone is appraised on the wrong basis or missed entirely

decisions.stage: Doing the work and gathering evidence
decisions.decision: Which leader evaluates an employee who changed roles mid-cycle
decisions.decidesOn: What work was performed in which role and for how long
decisions.basis: The new leader evaluates only the work performed in the new role for the remainder of the cycle
decisions.failurePath: Employee evaluated on work they no longer do, or on the wrong scope

decisions.stage: Getting the draft and talking it through
decisions.decision: The overall performance rating and narrative
decisions.decidesOn: Evidence of the year's work, the self-appraisal (if any), and the campus performance standards
decisions.basis: Human judgment against published standards; no AI-generated ratings
decisions.failurePath: Thin evidence (no self-appraisal, late drafting) produces a rating reconstructed from memory

decisions.decision: Whether the draft is ready to move to 'HR Review'
decisions.decidesOn: Whether the employee had their comment window and the draft is complete
decisions.basis: Leader is satisfied the appraisal is final and the employee has commented
decisions.failurePath: Moved early, before the employee's ≥5 business days, cutting off comment

systems.name: SPA (Staff Performance Appraisal)
systems.usedFor: Drafting, status workflow, self-appraisal, storing the appraisal of record

systems.name: UC Learning Center
systems.usedFor: Supervisor training ('Performance Management') and training-assignment verification

systems.name: Personnel file
systems.usedFor: System of record for the signed appraisal

breakingPoints.what: Optional self-appraisal often skipped, leaving the draft and rating thin
Draft invisible to the employee during 'HR Review', with no status surfaced back
Signature routing by email stalls the close against the deadline
Whole cycle bunches into the final two weeks before COB June 15
